articles.title: Code block font optimization: Fira Code CDN & font consistency fixes
articles.content:
Multiple fixes for code block font rendering:

- Load Fira Code from Google Fonts CDN (weights 400/600/700), no longer requiring local installation
- Fix dark mode code block background overriding highlight.js theme, making text invisible
- Remove non-monospace fonts (`DengXian`/`Noto Sans Mono CJK SC`) from the font stack to prevent proportional-width fallback for Latin characters
- Force all highlight `<span>` elements inside code blocks to inherit a unified `font-family`, eliminating font bifurcation at syntax highlight span boundaries
- Disable color variance for `.hljs-built_in` and other highlight classes inside code blocks to avoid jarring color differences mid-command

articles.title: GitHub Roast card dark mode support
articles.content:
GitHub Roast card now automatically switches theme with the site's dark mode:

- Added `id` attributes to card links and images in `index.html`, switched domain to `githubroast.dev`
- Added `updateGithubRoastTheme()` in `dark-mode.js` that toggles `?theme=dark` / `?theme=light` parameter
- Updates automatically on both manual dark mode toggle and system color scheme changes

articles.title: Fix leaderboard tab labels & exposed <strong> tags on public page
articles.content:
Fixed two display bugs:

- **Leaderboard tab labels** — `boardLabel()` constructed mismatched i18n keys (`tabPr`/`tabIssues`/`tabSubmissions` vs `tabPR`/`tabIssue`/`tabSubmission`), causing raw property names to be displayed. Switched to explicit key mapping.
- **Exposed `<strong>` tags on public page** — `applyI18n()` used `textContent` for `data-i18n` elements, which escaped HTML tags in translation values. Switched to `innerHTML`.

articles.title: Changelog split into User and Developer modes
articles.content:
The changelog page (`/change/`) now has a User/Developer dual-mode toggle:

- A new "User" / "Developer" switch at the top of the page
- **User mode** — hides chore entries, only shows feature updates and bug fixes
- **Developer mode** — shows all entries including chores
- Filter type buttons are automatically updated when switching modes
- Mode preference is saved in `localStorage` and persists across refreshes

articles.title: Add README docs and sync commit stats
articles.content:
- Add English README.md and Chinese README_CN.md project docs
- Update commit count (141→142) and push count (131→132) in `change/index.html`

articles.title: Restructure project directory layout
articles.content:
Reorganized project files into subdirectories by function:

- **JS files** — from flat `js/` into `js/core/`, `js/articles/`, `js/article-view/`
- **Data files** — `articles.json`, `articles-public.json`, `articles-change.json` moved to `data/`
- **Icon files** — `JS.svg` moved to `images/`
- Updated all `<script src>`, `<link rel="icon">`, `<meta og:image>` paths in HTML
- Updated all `fetch()` data paths in JS
- Updated `AGENTS.md` directory structure and path documentation

articles.title: Leaderboard PR/Issues now fetches from GitHub API
articles.content:
The leaderboard page (`/leaderboard/`) PR and Issues tabs now fetch real-time data from the GitHub API:

- **PR tab** — searches for repo PRs via `type:pr`, aggregates by creator, replaces hardcoded data
- **Issues tab** — searches for repo Issues via `type:issue`, excludes entries with `public-submission` and `stats:exclude` labels
- **Exclusion mechanism** — adding the `stats:exclude` label to an Issue excludes it from stats
- **Submissions excluded by default** — the submission template automatically carries the `public-submission` label
- Centralized configuration (`LEADERBOARD_CONFIG`) with customizable exclusion label lists

articles.title: Stop exposing contributor email addresses
articles.content:
Fix the issue where `authorLink` in public articles exposed contributor email addresses:

- Removed the `"authorLink": "mailto:..."` field from `articles-public.json`
- Added `sanitizeAuthorLink()` function in `utils.js` to automatically filter `mailto:` links
- All three author-link renderings in `articles-public.js` and `article-router.js` now use `sanitizeAuthorLink()`
- `mailto:` links no longer generate `<a>` tags, only plain author name is displayed
- Normal `https://` links are unaffected and render normally

articles.title: Changelog timeline now filterable by type
articles.content:
The changelog page (`/change/`) now supports type filtering:

- A filter button bar above the timeline, supporting "All", "Features", "Bug Fixes", and "Chores"
- Filter buttons have a fixed order with dynamic highlighting of the active selection
- Clicking a filter shows only matching entries without reloading the page

articles.title: New Leaderboard subpage
articles.content:
New `/leaderboard/` subpage with three rankings:

- **PR Leaderboard** — counted by number of contributions
- **Issues Leaderboard** — reserved for future use
- **Submissions Leaderboard** — dynamically aggregated by author from `articles-public.json`

Rankings support tab switching, entries are sorted alphabetically by name, and the top three display gold/silver/bronze badges.

articles.title: Project cleanup: SRI hash alignment, JS dedup, hamburger menu extraction
articles.content:
Comprehensive project file cleanup:

**SRI hash sync**
- Unified integrity values for marked, highlight.js, and pinyin-pro across `index.html`, `article.html`, `404.html`, and `public/index.html`

**Shared utility extraction**
- Added `js/utils.js` with a global `escapeHtml()`, eliminating duplicates in `article-content.js` and `articles-change.js`
- All pages load `utils.js` (after `display-mode.js`)
- `escapeHtml` in `articles-change.js` uses more robust type checking (`typeof str !== 'string'`)

**Hamburger menu unification**
- Added `initMobileMenu()` to `navigation.js`, encapsulating mobile menu toggle logic
- Removed duplicate inline hamburger code from `main.js`, `public/index.html`, `change/index.html`
- All pages now uniformly call `initMobileMenu()`

**Orphan file cleanup**
- Removed unused `images/logo.svg`

articles.title: Unified scrollbar style across the site
articles.content:
Unified scrollbar style across the site to a thin rounded-corner design:

- Added global `::-webkit-scrollbar` styles in `base.css` (6px width/height, rounded thumb)
- Added `--scrollbar-thumb` / `--scrollbar-thumb-hover` CSS variables for auto light/dark switching
- Added Firefox compatibility (`scrollbar-width: thin` + `scrollbar-color`)
- Removed duplicate scrollbar styles from `.modal-content` / `.search-results` in `overlay.css`
- All scrollable areas (article TOC sidebar etc.) automatically get the unified style

articles.title: Path unification: changelog → change
articles.content:
Renamed `changelog/` path and related files to `change/`:

- `changelog/` → `change/`
- `articles-changelog.json` → `articles-change.json`
- `js/articles-changelog.js` → `js/articles-change.js`
- `css/components/changelog.css` → `css/components/change.css`
- CSS class names and JS function names updated accordingly

articles.title: Search fixes: data timing / pinyin spaces / content pinyin / CDN SRI hashes
articles.content:
Fixed multiple search issues:

- **Data loading timing**: `initSearch()` was called before fetch completed, `dataSource` captured empty array. Fix: moved `initSearch()` call inside fetch `.then()` callback at the same level as `articlesData = data.articles`.
- **Pinyin space matching**: User input `"ru men"` (with space), but pinyin-pro outputs concatenated pinyin (`"rumen"`), causing `indexOf` to fail. Fix: in `findPinyinMatchRange()`, strip spaces from query with `replace(/\s+/g, '')` before comparison.
- **Missing content pinyin search**: The filter only matched pinyin against `article.title` and `article.excerpt`, but `article.content` only had direct text matching (`includes(q)`). Fix: added `matchPinyin(a.content.join('\n'), q)` to the filter.
- **All CDN scripts failing**: SRI integrity hashes for `marked`, `highlight.js`, and `pinyin-pro` didn't match actual CDN files. Fix: updated integrity attributes with computed SHAs from browser console errors.
- Also fixed SRI hashes for the same CDN scripts in `404.html`, `article.html`, and `change/index.html`.

articles.title: Add changelog subpage
articles.content:
Added a changelog subpage for recording blog feature updates and change history.

- New `change/` subpage for displaying changelog entries
- New "Changelog" link in navigation bar
- Added `articles-change.json` data file and `js/articles-change.js` rendering logic
- Added `css/components/change.css` stylesheet

articles.title: Modal sharing, search parameterization, security & accessibility improvements
articles.content:
Extracted modal as a shared module, search supports custom data source, unified XSS defense.

- Modal logic extracted into `article-modal-shared.js` + `article-modal.js`, reusable by subpages
- `initSearch()` supports `dataSource` / `onOpenArticle` parameters
- All output unified with `escapeHtml()` escaping to prevent XSS injection
- Search dialog added `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal` attributes
- All CDN resources now have SRI integrity checks and `crossorigin` attributes
- OG tags extracted into `updateOGTags()` / `restoreOGTags()` as standalone module
- CSS split into separate files: `cards.css`, `overlay.css`, `article.css` etc.

articles.title: Compatibility mode, Giscus dark theme, and other bug fixes
articles.content:
- Fixed public articles in compatibility mode (`article.html?id=pub-`) redirecting to main site
- Fixed Giscus comments not syncing theme when manually switching dark mode
- Fixed `navigation.js` incorrectly intercepting non-anchor links
- Changed relative path `/public/` → `public/` for correct local testing

articles.title: Reading settings: compatibility mode, mobile TOC improvements
articles.content:
- Added compatibility mode to reading settings (`article.html?id=`)
- Article TOC is now collapsible on mobile
- Fixed modal close button overlapping with content
- Added "Submit an Article" button in Hero area, linking to `/public/`
- Set `2029872426@qq.com` as the preferred submission email

articles.title: JSLY's Public Blog community submission system launched
articles.content:
The blog officially launched the community article submission system.

- New `/public/` standalone subpage for community articles
- Independent data source `articles-public.json` with `author` / `authorLink` fields
- Added `js/articles-public.js` for rendering submission cards (with author badges)
- Added `css/components/public.css` with distinct indigo theme
- Article detail page now shows author info bar
- Search supports public article data source
- `article-router.js` supports `/public/article/{id}` standalone page and `article.html?id=pub-{id}` compatibility route
- Users submit via GitHub Issue template; owner reviews and merges into `articles-public.json`

articles.title: Standalone article routing and 404 page
articles.content:
- Implemented SPA routing via 404.html with `/article/{id}` clean URLs
- Added `js/article-router.js` standalone routing module
- Unified 404 page style with suggestions (return to home, browse articles)
- Article pages and 404 page now have top navigation bar
- 404 page uses Flexbox sticky footer
- "Back to article list" button style improved on article pages

articles.title: JS/CSS modularization and architecture refactor
articles.content:
- JS split into 14 independent files by function
- CSS split into `base.css`, `layout.css`, `cards.css`, `overlay.css`, `article.css`, `responsive.css`
- Reading settings grouped into "Navigation Bar" and "Reading Mode" sections
- Added "Lock Navigation Bar" toggle
- Removed like button feature

articles.title: Reading progress bar moved into modal, dark mode improvements
articles.content:
- Reading progress bar moved from page top into article modal
- Fixed code highlighting double padding in dark mode, modal overflow, CSS animation conflicts
- Hero area background adapts to dark mode
- About Me Profile Card now uses `theme=dark` parameter
- Giscus comment area follows manual dark mode toggle
- Custom dark mode scrollbar styles
- Footer responsive layout with 480px breakpoint

articles.title: Giscus comment system integration
articles.content:
- Integrated Giscus comment system based on GitHub Discussions
- Used dynamic script injection instead of static embedding
- Giscus theme follows manual dark mode toggle
- Code font unified to Fira Code
- Removed tag filtering feature

articles.title: Syntax highlighting, RSS, dark mode, enhanced search, TOC navigation
articles.content:
- Integrated highlight.js 11.9.0 code highlighting
- Added `feed.xml` RSS subscription
- Added dark mode toggle
- Added pinyin search (based on pinyin-pro 3.26.0)
- Search results highlight matching keywords
- Chinese search requires minimum 2 characters
- Article auto-generates table of contents (TOC)
- Article reading progress bar
- Fixed 5 issues including Escape key listener leak and modal crash

articles.title: Article sharing, OG tags, and hash deep links
articles.content:
- Added share button on article detail page with link copy support
- Dynamic Open Graph tag updates
- Supports `#article-{id}` hash anchor deep links
- Added `support@blog.jsly.asia` contact email in footer

articles.title: Content system and architecture refactor
articles.content:
- Changed `content` field in `articles.json` to an array of strings
- CSS reorganized into `base.css`, `layout.css`, `components/` directory
- Added `pinned` field for pinning articles
- Added `showImage` field, each article can independently control image display

articles.title: Social links integration
articles.content:
Footer social links connected to: QQ, GitHub, Luogu, Luogu.me, AtCoder, Qzone.

- Social icons replaced with standalone SVG files
- Logo changed to pure graphic SVG text

articles.title: AGPL-3.0 open source license and copyright notices
articles.content:
- Added AGPL-3.0 license to the repository
- All source files (HTML/CSS/JS) now have SPDX copyright headers
- Added source code link in footer to comply with AGPL-3.0 requirements

articles.title: Search feature launched
articles.content:
- Search icon in top-right corner, click to open search panel
- Matching against title, tags, excerpt, and full content
- Real-time search with instant feedback
- Fixed page load errors, empty images, navigation highlight, and other bugs
- Added Chinese fallback font and SEO tags

articles.title: Article system and design theme launched
articles.content:
- Article content supports Markdown rendering (via marked.js)
- Removed categories, switched to tag-based system with flat article layout
- Changed overall theme to light green fresh style
- Hero area switched from gradient to solid color background
- Removed sidebar, full-width content layout
- About Me section now displays Profile Card SVG
- Blog title changed to "JSLY Blog"
- Added OpenCode usage guide article

articles.title: Site initialization
articles.content:
JSLY's Blog is officially online!

- Configured custom domain www.jsly.asia (CNAME record)
- Deployed via GitHub Pages
- Pure static HTML/CSS/JS, no build tools

articles.title: Security fixes: XSS protection + isSafeUrl utility + code quality improvements
articles.content:
- `sanitizeAuthorLink()` switched to whitelist mode (only http/https allowed)
- Added `isSafeUrl()` / `resolveArticleImageUrl()` utility functions
- Fixed XSS injection risk in article image `src` and OG image URLs
- Fixed missing `escapeHtml()` for `article.date`/`views`/`readTime` in `articles-public.js`
- Fixed public article share URL (was incorrectly using `/article/`)
- Removed fragile string `.replace()` author injection, using function parameter instead
- `isSafeUrl()` changed to blacklist mode, blocking `javascript:`/`data:`/`file:` protocols
- CSS: Added `scroll-margin-top` to prevent fixed header from covering anchor targets
- Added `<meta name="theme-color">` to all HTML files
- Cleaned up orphaned `data/articles.json` and `data/articles-public.json`
